summary: Package exfiltrates host and credential data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package ships an install/lifecycle hook in package.json (line 30) and a scanner.js module that together harvest host identity (os.hostname(), whoami via execSync), environment variables, current working directory, and filesystem contents, then POST the collected data to a hardcoded endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js imports fs, os, https, and child_process at the top, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync checks consistent with credential-path enumeration, executes shell commands (whoami, curl), and issues https.request POSTs and https.get calls to the same hardcoded GitHub Pages host. The GitHub Pages domain (ddjidd564.github.io) is an anonymous personal account unrelated to any legitimate publisher and serves as an attacker-controlled C2/exfil sink. The package's stated purpose ("crypto-credential-scanner") is a cover-story for a credential harvester: rather than scanning the caller's data locally, it ships the harvested data off-host to a third-party endpoint. Installing or loading this package causes installer-side host identifiers, environment variables, and filesystem-derived secrets to be transmitted to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
